seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Google ADK, Tool Loop Detection issues arise when agents enter infinite cycles of tool invocations, particularly in multi-agent workflows where delegation creates circular dependencies. Gemini models may repeatedly call the same tools due to inconsistent parsing of tool responses or session state drift in long conversations. This manifests as stalled agent execution, excessive API calls, and corrupted session states, crippling multi-agent systems.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent A delegates to Agent B, which delegates back to Agent A with identical tool calls
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loop consuming Gemini API quota and session memory

howItManifests.scenario: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
howItManifests.symptom: Tool responses from gemini-1.5-pro parsed incorrectly by gemini-2.0-flash
howItManifests.impact: Repeated tool calls with malformed parameters

howItManifests.scenario: Session state corruption in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Cached tool responses accumulate causing state bloat
howItManifests.impact: Context window overflow and agent hallucination

howItManifests.scenario: Tool response format mismatches across agents
howItManifests.symptom: One agent returns JSON, another expects YAML-formatted tool output

detection.name: Tool Call Frequency Monitor
detection.description: Tracks identical tool calls within session using ADK's execution history

detection.name: Agent Delegation Cycle Detector
detection.description: Monitors cross-agent delegation patterns in multi-agent workflows

detection.name: Session State Bloat Monitor
detection.description: Detects excessive tool response accumulation in session state

fixes.name: Tool Call Deduplication Middleware
fixes.description: Prevents repeated identical tool calls using session cache

fixes.name: Delegation Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Limits recursive delegation depth in multi-agent workflows

fixes.name: State Pruning Callback
fixes.description: Automatically prunes old tool responses from session state

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK, automatically detecting tool loops across multi-agent workflows. It monitors tool call patterns, delegation cycles, and session state in real-time, providing precise loop detection with zero configuration. Vex not only identifies the problematic agents and tools but also applies intelligent remediation - from call deduplication to circuit breaking - while preserving legitimate agent behavior.

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) provides production-grade observability with automatic root cause analysis, showing exactly which Gemini model outputs triggered loops and which tool parameters repeated. Integration takes 3 lines of code and works across all ADK agent types, catching 95% of tool loops before they consume API quota.

Vex's adaptive remediation learns from your specific agent patterns, automatically tuning detection thresholds and fix strategies without manual intervention.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from google_adk import Agent, before_agent_callback

# 3-line Vex integration
vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')

@vex.monitor('tool-loop-detection')
@before_agent_callback
def adk_agent_with_vex(ctx):
    # Vex automatically detects loops and applies fixes
    ctx.proceed()

# Attach to your ADK agent
agent = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gemini-1.5-pro',
    callbacks=[adk_agent_with_vex]
)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk google-adk` and eliminate tool loops in your ADK agents forever.
